search_radius.description: The search radius, in meters, when trying to match each trace point.

gps_accuracy.description: The accuracy of the GPS, in meters.

breakage_distance.description: The breaking distance, in meters, between trace points.

interpolation_distance.description: The interpolation distance, in meters, beyond which trace points are merged together.

turn_penalty_factor.description: Penalizes turns from one road segment to next. For a pedestrian trace, you may see a back-and-forth motion along the streets of your path with the default settings. Try increasing the turn penalty factor to 500 to reduce jitter in the output. Note that if GPS accuracy is already good, increasing this above the default will usually negatively affect the quality of map matching.

time.description: The timestamp of the waypoint, in seconds. This can inform the map matching algorithm about when the point was measured. A UNIX timestamp, or any increasing integer sequence measuring seconds from some reference point can be used.

lat.description: The latitude of the matched point.

lon.description: The longitude of the matched point.

edge_index.description: The index of the edge in the list of `edges`. This key will be missing if the point is unmatched.

begin_route_discontinuity.description: If true, this match result is the begin location of a route discontinuity.

end_route_discontinuity.description: If true, this match result is the end location of a route discontinuity.

distance_along_edge.description: The distance along the associated edge for this matched point, expressed as a value between 0 and 1. For example, if the matched point is halfway along the edge, then the value will be 0.5. This key will be absent if the point is unmatched.

distance_from_trace_point.description: The distance in meters from the trace point to the matched point. This key will be absent if the point is unmatched.

distance.description:
The distance (in `units`) between the location in `sources` at `from_index` and the location in `targets` at `to_index`.
This value may be 0 in the case that the source and destination are the same, and `null` if no route was found between the locations.

time.description:
The travel time (in seconds) between the location in `sources` at `from_index` and the location in `targets` at `to_index`.
This value may be 0 in the case that the source and destination are the same, and `null` if no route was found between the locations.

from_index.description: The index of the start location in the `sources` array.

to_index.description: The index of the end location in the `targets` array.

matrix_locations.description: Only applicable to one-to-many or many-to-one requests. This defaults to all locations. When specified explicitly, this option allows a partial result to be returned. This is basically equivalent to "find the closest/best locations out of the full set." This can have a dramatic improvement for large requests.

sources.description: The list of starting locations determined by snapping to the nearest appropriate point on the road network for the costing model. All locations appear in the same order as the input.

targets.description: The list of ending locations determined by snapping to the nearest appropriate point on the road network for the costing model. All locations appear in the same order as the input.

sources_to_targets.description: The matrix of starting and ending locations, along with the computed distance and travel time. The array is row-ordered. This means that the time and distance from the first location to all others forms the first row of the array, followed by the time and distance from the second source location to all target locations, etc.

search_cutoff.description: The cutoff (in meters) at which we will assume the input is too far away from civilisation to be worth correlating to the nearest graph elements. The default is 35 km.

use_primary.description: A measure of willingness to use primary roads. Values near 0 attempt to avoid primary roads and stay on roads with lower speeds, and values near 1 indicate the rider is more comfortable on these roads.

use_hills.description: A measure of willingness to take tackle hills. Values near 0 attempt to avoid hills and steeper grades even if it means a longer route, and values near 1 indicates that the rider does not fear them. Note that as some routes may be impossible without hills, 0 does not guarantee avoidance of them.

use_highways.description: A measure of willingness to use highways. Values near 0 attempt to avoid highways and stay on roads with lower speeds, and values near 1 indicate the rider is more comfortable on these roads.

use_trails.description: A measure of the rider's sense of adventure. Values near 0 attempt to avoid highways and stay on roads with potentially unsuitable terrain (trails, tracks, unclassified, or bad surfaces), and values near 1 will tend to avoid major roads and route on secondary roads.

locations.description: The list of locations. The first and last are assumed to be the start and end points, and all intermediate points are locations that you want to visit along the way.

iso_3166_1.description: The ISO 3166-1 two-character code for the admin region.

iso_3166_1_alpha3.description: The ISO 3166-1 three-character code for the admin region.

distance.description: The distance, in meters, between each pair of coordinates.

duration.description: The duration between each pair of coordinates, in seconds.

speed.description: The estimated speed of travel between each pair of coordinates in meters/sec.

components.description: A list of objects with additional context that allow for visual layout improvements beyond what's possible with plain text.

distanceAlongGeometry.description: How far (in meters) from the upcoming maneuver the instruction should start being displayed.

osrmGuidanceModifier.description: An optional value indicating the directional change of the maneuver (further clarifying type).

osrmIntersection.description: Detailed information about intersections that the route traverses. For every step, the first intersection is at the location of the maneuver. Additional intersections will be provided for every road or path traversed until the next step.

bearings.description: A list of bearing values that are available for travel through the intersection.

items.description: The classes of roads exiting the intersection.

entry.description: A list of entry flags, which map 1:1 to the bearings. A value of true indicates that the respective road could be entered on a valid route. False indicates that the turn onto the respective road would violate a restriction.

in.description: An index into bearings/entry array. Used to calculate the bearing just before the turn. Namely, the clockwise angle from true north to the direction of travel immediately before the maneuver/passing the intersection. Bearings are given relative to the intersection. To get the bearing in the direction of driving, the bearing has to be rotated by a value of 180. The value is not supplied for depart maneuvers.

out.description: An index into bearings/entry array. Used to calculate the bearing just after the turn. Namely, the clockwise angle from true north to the direction of travel immediately after the maneuver/passing the intersection. This is not supplied for arrive maneuvers.

lanes.description: Available turn lanes at the intersection. May be omitted if no lane information is available for the intersection.

admin_index.description: The index into the admin boundaries list on the route leg.

duration.description: The estimated duration, in seconds, to traverse the intersection.

turn_duration.description: The estimated duration, in seconds, to complete the turn.

geometry_index.description: The index of the intersection in the leg geometry.

indications.description: A list of indication (e.g. marking on the road) specifying the turn lane. A road can have multiple indications (e.g. an arrow pointing straight and left).

valid.description: True if the lane is a valid choice for the current maneuver.

distance.description: The distance traveled by the route, in meters.

duration.description: The estimated travel time, in number of seconds.

geometry.description: An encoded polyline (https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm). Note that the polyline is encoded with 6 digits of precision rather than the default 5!

weight.description: The total cost of the route computed by the routing engine.

weight_name.description: The costing model used for the route.

distance.description: The distance traveled by the route, in meters.

duration.description: The estimated travel time, in number of seconds.

weight.description: The total cost of the leg computed by the routing engine.

via_waypoints.description: Indicates which waypoints are passed through rather than creating a new leg.

admins.description: Administrative regions visited along the leg.

osrmRouteStep.description: A maneuver such as a turn or merge, followed by travel along a single road or path.

distance.description: The distance traveled by the route, in meters.

duration.description: The estimated travel time, in number of seconds.

geometry.description: An encoded polyline (https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm) with 6 digits of decimal precision (NOTE: most implementations default to 5).

name.description: The name of the segment (ex: road) being traversed

ref.description: A reference number of code for the segment being traversed.

pronunciation.description: Pronunciation of the name (if available). The format of this varies by implementation/vendor.

rotary_name.description: The name of the traffic circle.

rotary_pronunciation.description: Pronunciation of the rotary name (if available). The format of this varies by implementation/vendor.

driving_side.description: The side of the road on which driving is legal for this step.

voiceInstructions.description: A list of announcements which should be spoken at various points along the maneuver.

bannerInstructions.description: A list of announcements which should be displayed prominently on screen at various points along the maneuver.

speedLimitSign.description: The style of speed limit signs used along the step.

speedLimitUnit.description: The unit of measure that is used locally along the step. This may be different from the unit used in maxspeed annotations, and is provided so that apps can localize their display.

osrmSpeedLimit.description: The speed limit between the pair of coordinates.

unit.description: The unit of measure for the speed. Always included if speed is present.

unknown.description: True if the speed limit is not known.

none.description: True if there is no explicit speed limit (ex: some Autobahn sections)

instruction.description: A human-readable instruction for the maneuver.

bearing_before.description: The clockwise angle from true north to the direction of travel immediately before the maneuver.

bearing_after.description: The clockwise angle from true north to the direction of travel immediately after the maneuver.

exit.description: The exit number to take (for roundabouts, rotaries, and number of intersections).

distance_from_start.description: The distance from the start of the leg, in meters.

geometry_index.description: The index of the waypoint's location in the route geometry.

waypoint_index.description: The index of the associated waypoint.

distanceAlongGeometry.description: How far (in meters) from the upcoming maneuver the instruction should be announced.

ssmlAnnouncement.description: The announcement in Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML). Supported TTS engines include Amazon Polly and Apple's AVSpeechSynthesizer.

distance.description: The distance of the snapped point from the original location.

walkway_factor.description: A factor that multiplies the cost when walkways are encountered.

sidewalk_factor.description: A factor that multiplies the cost when sidewalks are encountered.

alley_factor.description: A factor that multiplies the cost when alleys are encountered.

driveway_factor.description: A factor that multiplies the cost when driveways are encountered.

step_penalty.description: A penalty (in seconds) added to each transition onto a path with steps or stairs.

use_lit.description: A measure of preference for streets that are lit. 0 indicates indifference toward lit streets, and 1 indicates that unlit streets should be avoided. Note that even with values near 1, there is no guarantee that the returned route will include lit segments.

max_hiking_difficulty.description: The maximum difficulty of hiking trails allowed. This corresponds to the OSM `sac_scale`.

bss_rent_cost.description: The estimated cost (in seconds) to rent a bicycle from a sharing station in `bikeshare` mode.

bss_rent_penalty.description: A penalty (in seconds) to rent a bicycle in `bikeshare` mode.

pedestrianType.description: If set to blind, enables additional route instructions which are helpful for blind users. These include announcing street crossings, stairs, bridges, tunnels, gates, and bollards passed on the route. If set to wheelchair, the walking speed and step penalty are adjusted for wheelchair users.

placeIDs.description: A list of GIDs to search for.

requestId.description: An identifier to disambiguate requests (echoed by the server).

reverseLat.description: The latitude of the point at which to perform the search.

reverseLon.description: The longitude of the point at which to perform the search.

roadClass.description: Class of road (ranked in descending order)

shape.description: An encoded polyline (https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm) with 6 digits of decimal precision (NOTE: Most implementations default to 5!).

elevation_interval.description: The sampling distance between elevation values along the route. This echoes the request parameter having the same name (converted to `units` if necessary).

elevation.description: An array of elevation values sampled every `elevation_interval`. Units are either metric or imperial depending on the value of `units`.

type.description:
The type of route maneuver.

| Code | Type                                |
|------|-------------------------------------|
| 0    | None                                |
| 1    | Start                               |
| 2    | Start right                         |
| 3    | Start left                          |
| 4    | Destination                         |
| 5    | Destination right                   |
| 6    | Destination left                    |
| 7    | Becomes                             |
| 8    | Continue                            |
| 9    | Slight right                        |
| 10   | Right                               |
| 11   | Sharp right                         |
| 12   | U-turn right                        |
| 13   | U-turn left                         |
| 14   | Sharp left                          |
| 15   | Left                                |
| 16   | Slight left                         |
| 17   | Ramp straight                       |
| 18   | Ramp right                          |
| 19   | Ramp left                           |
| 20   | Exit right                          |
| 21   | Exit left                           |
| 22   | Stay straight                       |
| 23   | Stay right                          |
| 24   | Stay left                           |
| 25   | Merge                               |
| 26   | Enter roundabout                    |
| 27   | Exit roundabout                     |
| 28   | Enter ferry                         |
| 29   | Exit ferry                          |
| 30   | Transit                             |
| 31   | Transit transfer                    |
| 32   | Transit remain on                   |
| 33   | Transit connection start            |
| 34   | Transit connection transfer         |
| 35   | Transit connection destination      |
| 36   | Post-transit connection destination |
| 37   | Merge right                         |
| 38   | Merge left                          |

verbal_transition_alert_instruction.description: Text suitable for use as a verbal navigation alert.

verbal_pre_transition_instruction.description: Text suitable for use as a verbal navigation alert immediately prior to the maneuver transition.

verbal_post_transition_instruction.description: Text suitable for use as a verbal navigation alert immediately after to the maneuver transition.

street_names.description: A list of street names that are consistent along the entire maneuver.

begin_street_names.description: A list of street names at the beginning of the maneuver, if they are different from the names at the end.

time.description: The estimated time to complete the entire maneuver, in seconds.

length.description: The length of the maneuver, in `units`.

begin_shape_index.description: The index into the list of shape points for the start of the maneuver.

end_shape_index.description: The index into the list of shape points for the end of the maneuver.

toll.description: True any portion of the maneuver is subject to a toll.

rough.description: True any portion of the maneuver is unpaved or has portions of rough pavement.

gate.description: True if a gate is encountered in the course of this maneuver.

ferry.description: True if a ferry is encountered in the course of this maneuver.

roundabout_exit_count.description: The exit number of the roundabout to take after entering.

depart_instruction.description: The written departure time instruction (typically used in a transit maneuver).

verbal_depart_instruction.description: Text suitable for use as a verbal departure time instruction (typically used in a transit maneuver).
verbal_depart_instruction.example: Depart at 8:04 AM from Seoul Station

arrive_instruction.description: The written arrival time instruction (typically used in a transit maneuver).

verbal_arrive_instruction.description: Text suitable for use as a verbal departure time instruction (typically used in a transit maneuver).
verbal_arrive_instruction.example: Arrive at 8:06 AM at City Hall

verbal_multi_cue.description: True if the `verbal_pre_transition_instruction` has been appended with the verbal instruction of the next maneuver.

travel_type.description: The type of travel over the maneuver. This can be thought of as a specialization of the travel mode. For example, vehicular travel may be via car, motorcycle, etc.; and travel via bicycle may be via a road bike, mountain bike, etc.

bss_maneuver_type.description: Describes a bike share action when using bikeshare routing.

exclude_locations.description: This has the same format as the locations list. Locations are mapped to the closest road(s), and these road(s) are excluded from the route path computation.

exclude_polygons.description: One or multiple exterior rings of polygons in the form of nested JSON arrays. Roads intersecting these rings will be avoided during path finding. Open rings will be closed automatically. If you only need to avoid a few specific roads, it's much more efficient to use `exclude_locations`.

alternates.description: How many alternate routes are desired. Note that fewer or no alternates may be returned. Alternates are not yet supported on routes with more than 2 locations or on time-dependent routes.

roundabout_exits.description: Determines whether the output should include roundabout exit instructions.

maneuvers.verbal_pre_transition_instruction: Drive west on Alaska 1, Seward Highway. Then You will arrive at your destination.

maneuvers.instruction: You have arrived at your destination.
maneuvers.verbal_pre_transition_instruction: You have arrived at your destination.
maneuvers.verbal_transition_alert_instruction: You will arrive at your destination.

time.description: The estimated travel time, in seconds

length.description: The estimated travel distance, in `units` (km or mi)

min_lat.description: The minimum latitude of the bounding box containing the route.

max_lat.description: The maximum latitude of the bounding box containing the route.

min_lon.description: The minimum longitude of the bounding box containing the route.

max_lon.description: The maximum longitude of the bounding box containing the route.

original_index.description: The original index of the location (locations may be reordered for optimized routes)

heading.description:
The preferred direction of travel when starting the route, in integer clockwise degrees from north. North is 0, south is 180, east is 90, and west is 270.
Avoid specifying this unless you really know what you're doing. Most use cases for this are better served by `preferred_side`.

heading_tolerance.description: The tolerance (in degrees) determining whether a street is considered the same direction.

minimum_reachability.description: The minimum number of nodes that must be reachable for a given edge to consider that edge as belonging to a connected region. If a candidate edge has fewer connections, it will be considered a disconnected island.

radius.description:
The distance (in meters) to look for candidate edges around the location for purposes of snapping locations to the route graph. If there are no candidates within this distance, the closest candidate within a reasonable search distance will be used. This is subject to clamping by internal limits.
This allows the routing engine to match another edge which is NOT the closest to your location, but in within this range. This can be useful if you have other constraints and want to disambiguate, but beware that this can lead to very strange results, particularly if you have specified other parameters like `heading`. This is an advanced feature and should only be used with extreme care.

rank_candidates.description: If true, candidates will be ranked according to their distance from the target location as well as other factors. If false, candidates will only be ranked using their distance from the target.

preferred_side.description: If the location is not offset from the road centerline or is closest to an intersection, this option has no effect. Otherwise, the preferred side of street is used to determine whether or not the location should be visited from the same, opposite or either side of the road with respect to the side of the road the given locale drives on.

node_snap_tolerance.description: During edge correlation this is the tolerance (in meters) used to determine whether or not to snap to the intersection rather than along the street, if the snap location is within this distance from the intersection, the intersection is used instead.

street_side_tolerance.description: A tolerance in meters from the edge centerline used for determining the side of the street that the location is on. If the distance to the centerline is less than this tolerance, no side will be inferred. Otherwise, the left or right side will be selected depending on the direction of travel.

street_side_max_distance.description: The max distance in meters that the input coordinates or display lat/lon can be from the edge centerline for them to be used for determining the side of the street. Beyond this distance, no street side is inferred.

searchAddress.description: A street name, optionally with a house number.

searchBorough.description: A unit within a city (not widely used, but present in places like NYC and Mexico City).

searchBoundaryCircleLat.description: The latitude of the center of a circle to limit the search to. Requires `boundary.circle.lon`.

searchBoundaryCircleLon.description: The longitude of the center of a circle to limit the search to. Requires `boundary.circle.lat`.

searchBoundaryCircleRadius.description: The radius of the circle (in kilometers) to limit the search to. Defaults to 50km if unspecified.

searchBoundaryCountry.description: A list of country codes in ISO 3116-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 format.

searchBoundaryGID.description: The GID of an area to limit the search to.

searchBoundaryRectMaxLat.description: Defines the max latitude component of a bounding box to limit the search to. Requires all other `boundary.rect` parameters to be specified.

searchBoundaryRectMaxLon.description: Defines the max longitude component of a bounding box to limit the search to. Requires all other `boundary.rect` parameters to be specified.

searchBoundaryRectMinLat.description: Defines the min latitude component of a bounding box to limit the search to. Requires all other `boundary.rect` parameters to be specified.

searchBoundaryRectMinLon.description: Defines the min longitude component of a bounding box to limit the search to. Requires all other `boundary.rect` parameters to be specified.

searchCountry.description: A country code in ISO 3116-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 format.

searchCounty.description: Administrative divisions between localities and regions. Not commonly used as input to structured geocoding.

searchFocusLat.description: The latitude of the point to focus the search on. This will bias results toward the focus point. Requires `focus.point.lon`.

searchFocusLon.description: The longitude of the point to focus the search on. This will bias results toward the focus point. Requires `focus.point.lat`.

searchLayers.description: A list of layers to limit the search to.

searchLocality.description: The city, village, town, etc. that the place/address is part of.

searchNeighborhood.description: Varies by area, but has a locally specific meaning (NOT always an official administrative unit).

searchRegion.description: Typically the first administrative division within a country. For example, a US state or a Canadian province.

searchSources.description: A list of sources to limit the search to.

searchText.description: The place name (address, venue name, etc.) to search for.

serviceFactorCostingOption.description: A factor that multiplies the cost when service roads are encountered. The default is 1.2 for cars and busses, and 1 for trucks, motor scooters, and motorcycles.

servicePenaltyCostingOption.description: A penalty applied to transitions to service roads. This penalty can be used to reduce the likelihood of suggesting a route with service roads unless absolutely necessary. The default penalty is 15 for cars, busses, motor scooters, and motorcycles; and zero for others.

type.description: A `break` represents the start or end of a leg, and allows reversals. A `through` location is an intermediate waypoint that must be visited between `break`s, but at which reversals are not allowed. A `via` is similar to a `through` except that reversals are allowed. A `break_through` is similar to a `break` in that it can be the start/end of a leg, but does not allow reversals. Defaults to `break`.

predicted.description: Does this edge have predicted (historical) speed records?

constrained_flow.description: Speed when there is no traffic, in kph.

free_flow.description: Speed when there is heavy traffic, in kph.

type.description: The type of speed which is used when setting default speeds. When `tagged`, the explicit `max_speed` tags from OpenStreetMap are being used. When `classified`, the values are being inferred from the highway classification.

default.description: The default speed used for calculations. NOTE: Values greater than 250 are used for special cases and should not be treated as literal.

action.description: Determines whether the list of attributes will be used as a whitelist or a blacklist.

edges.description: The list of edges matched along the path.

admins.description: The set of administrative regions matched along the path. Rather than repeating this information for every end node, the admins in this list are referenced by index.

matched_points.description: List of match results when using the map_snap shape match algorithm. There is a one-to-one correspondence with the input set of latitude, longitude coordinates and this list of match results.

shape.description: The encoded polyline (https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm) of the matched path.

filters.description: If present, provides either a whitelist or a blacklist of keys to include/exclude in the response. This key is optional, and if omitted from the request, all available info will be returned.

alternate_paths.description: Alternate paths, if any, that were not classified as the best match.

names.description: The name(s) of the road at this edge, if any.

length.description: The length of this edge in `units`.

speed.description: The speed of this edge in `units`/hr, in terms of average/free-flow speed for routing purposes. This is affected by any number of factors such as the road service, vehicle type, etc. and not just the posted speed limits.

begin_heading.description: The direction at the beginning of an edge. The units are degrees clockwise from north.

end_heading.description: The direction at the end of an edge. The units are degrees clockwise from north.

begin_shape_index.description: Index into the list of shape points for the start of the edge.

end_shape_index.description: Index into the list of shape points for the end of the edge.

toll.description: True if the edge has a toll.

unpaved.description: True if the edge has rough payment.

tunnel.description: True if the edge is a tunnel.

bridge.description: True if the edge is a bridge.

roundabout.description: True if the edge is a roundabout.

internal_intersection.description: True if the edge is an internal intersection.

drive_on_right.description: True if the edge is in an area where you must drive on the right side of the road.

surface.description: The type of surface for the edge.

way_id.description: The way identifier of the edge in OSM.

weighted_grade.description: The weighted grade factor. The weighted grade is derived from elevation data. It is a measure used for hill avoidance in routing - sort of a relative energy use along an edge. But since an edge can possibly go up and down over several hills, it might not equate to what you would normally think of as grade.

max_upward_grade.description: The maximum upward slope. A value of 32768 indicates no elevation data is available for this edge.

max_downward_grade.description: The maximum downward slope. A value of 32768 indicates no elevation data is available for this edge.

mean_elevation.description: The mean elevation along the edge. Units are meters by default. If the `units` are specified as miles, then the mean elevation is returned in feet. A value of 32768 indicates no elevation data is available for this edge.

lane_count.description: The number of lanes for this edge.

cycle_lane.description: The type of cycle lane (if any) along this edge.

bicycle_network.description:
The type of bicycle network, if any. This is an integer comprised of constants bitwise or'd together. For example, a route that's part of both a local and mountain network would have a value of 12.
1 - National 2 - Regional 4 - Local 8 - Mountain

sac_scale.description:
The difficulty of the hiking trail according to the SAC scale.
0 - No Sac Scale 1 - Hiking 2 - Mountain hiking 3 - Demanding mountain hiking 4 - Alpine hiking 5 - Demanding alpine hiking 6 - Difficult alpine hiking

speed_limit.description: The speed limit along the edge measured in `units`/hr. This may be either an integer or the string "unlimited" if speed limit data is available. If absent, there is no speed limit data available.

truck_speed.description: The truck speed of this edge in `units`/hr, in terms of average/free-flow speed for routing purposes. This is affected by any number of factors such as the road service, vehicle type, etc. and not just the posted speed limits.

truck_route.description: True if the edge is part of a truck route/network.

traversability.description: The directions in which the edge is traversable.

height.description: The height of the truck (in meters).

width.description: The width of the truck (in meters).

length.description: The length of the truck (in meters).

weight.description: The weight of the truck (in tonnes).

axle_load.description: The axle load of the truck (in tonnes).

hazmat.description: Whether or not the truck is carrying hazardous materials.

tz_id.description:
The canonical time zone ID.

In the event that multiple time zones could be returned,
the first one from the Unicode CLDR timezone.xml is returned.

base_utc_offset.description: The base offset, in seconds, from UTC that is normally in effect for this time zone.

dst_offset.description:
The special offset, in seconds, from UTC that is in effect for this time zone as of the queried timestamp (defaults to now).

If no additional offsets are in effect, this value is zero.
This typically reflects Daylight Saving Time, but may indicate other special offsets.
To get the total offset in effect, add `dst_offset` and `utc_offset` together.

NOTE: This field can be somewhat confusing.
We recommend switching to the v2 endpoint and using the is_dst filed if you need to identify whether DST is being observed.

timestamp.description:
Integer non-leap seconds since January 1, 1970 (UNIX timestamp).

If present, offsets will be calculated as of this time.
Otherwise, offsets will be effective as of the time of the query.

local_rfc_2822_timestamp.description:
The local time expressed as an RFC 2822 timestamp (e.g. Tue, 1 Jun 2003 10:52:37 -0500).

If a timestamp is included in the request, it will be localized here.
Otherwise, this will reflect the time of the request.

NOTE: RFC 2822 is more restrictive than other formats and cannot represent all dates.

local_rfc_3389_timestamp.description:
The local time expressed as an RFC 3389 (ISO 8601) timestamp (e.g. 2003-06-01T10:52:37+02:00).

If a timestamp is included in the request, it will be localized here.
Otherwise, this will reflect the time of the request.

tz_id.description:
The canonical time zone ID.

In the event that multiple time zones could be returned,
the first one from the Unicode CLDR timezone.xml is returned.

utc_offset.description:
The total offset, in seconds, from UTC that is currently in effect for this time zone.

This accounts for both the standard offset and any seasonal adjustments (e.g. DST).

is_dst.description:
Whether Daylight Saving Time (or a similar seasonal offset) is in effect
at the queried timestamp.

timestamp.description:
Integer non-leap seconds since January 1, 1970 (UNIX timestamp).

If present, offsets will be calculated as of this time.
Otherwise, offsets will be effective as of the time of the query.

local_rfc_2822_timestamp.description:
The local time expressed as an RFC 2822 timestamp (e.g. Tue, 1 Jun 2003 10:52:37 -0500).

If a timestamp is included in the request, it will be localized here.
Otherwise, this will reflect the time of the request.

NOTE: RFC 2822 is more restrictive than other formats and cannot represent all dates.

local_rfc_3339_timestamp.description:
The local time expressed as an RFC 3339 (ISO 8601) timestamp (e.g. 2003-06-01T10:52:37+02:00).

If a timestamp is included in the request, it will be localized here.
Otherwise, this will reflect the time of the request.

useFerryCostingOption.description: A measure of willingness to take ferries. Values near 0 attempt to avoid ferries, and values near 1 will favour them. Note that as some routes may be impossible without ferries, 0 does not guarantee avoidance of them.

useHillsCostingOption.description: A measure of willingness to take tackle hills. Values near 0 attempt to avoid hills and steeper grades even if it means a longer route, and values near 1 indicates that the user does not fear them. Note that as some routes may be impossible without hills, 0 does not guarantee avoidance of them.

useLivingStreetsCostingOption.description: A measure of willingness to take living streets. Values near 0 attempt to avoid them, and values near 1 will favour them. Note that as some routes may be impossible without living streets, 0 does not guarantee avoidance of them. The default value is 0 for trucks; 0.1 for other motor vehicles; 0.5 for bicycles; and 0.6 for pedestrians.

useTracksCostingOption.description: A measure of willingness to take track roads. Values near 0 attempt to avoid them, and values near 1 will favour them. Note that as some routes may be impossible without track roads, 0 does not guarantee avoidance of them. The default value is 0 for automobiles, busses, and trucks; and 0.5 for all other costing modes.
